entry.title: Variance to previous year on a year-to-date line chart compares current month fully not partially
entry.content:
Hi Folks,

I got some revenue data on daily level that I show monthly aggregated in a line chart filtered on Year-to-date.

I enabled varance to see difference / Year-over-Year comparison. Working fine in general except for the current month.

There the variance is comparing to "complete month" of the previous year instead of the to-date time intervall of that month.  My expectation would be that when running story today the variance would compare 06/01/2025 ... 06/17/2025   to 06/01/2024 ... 06/17/2024 time intervals for the current month.

Do I do something wrong? 

Thanks and best regards
Axel

entry.title: How to release a delivery block in S/4HANA Service Advanced Shipment Scenario?
entry.content:
Hello everyone, 

we are using S/4HANA Service Release 2023 FP 02 Private Cloud and use advanced shipment logistics scenario (item type - SRVV). In standard configuration it is not possible to maintain a sales order created from service order via e.g. VA02. It is necessary to maintain changes in service order which get updated to related sales order. 

However it is common practice to customize delivery blocks on sales orders based on business partner customer status settings. Now it is not possible for us to manually release the sales order dlivery block on header. We can only manipulate the delivery block on item level which is not sufficient. 

We have tried adding new fields for delivery block on shipping view of service order header but it didn't solve our issue. Even Sales Order Fiori Apps aren't able to release the delivery block with well known error message V4 310.

Do you have any ideas what is recommended in standard? It seems like a soft lock o us in the current environment. 

Maybe @Silvia_Ventura knows a solution?

entry.title: Re: SearchHelp - Public Cloud Generate UI Service Table
entry.content:
Hi Enry,

Great question, you're diving into a common challenge when using custom domains with fixed values, and a standard value helps in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Here are the recommended ways:

1st option, ABAP Development Tools (ADT) in Eclipse

  • Use Ctrl+Shift+A to search for I_*VH or C_*VH (value help views).
  • Look for released CDS views with the annotation

2nd option, SAP Help / API Hub

  • Visit: https://api.sap.com
  • Search for “value help” or entities like I_CompanyCodeStdVH in the S/4HANA Cloud section.
  • These often contain a list of released CDS views for public use.

3rd option, SAP Explorer for S/4HANA Cloud (Cloud SDK)

  • SAP’s “View Browser” app (Fiori) in Public Cloud allows you to browse released CDS views.
  • Filter by usage type: External API / Released View.

https://help.sap.com/docs/BTP/abap-cds-consumption-annotations

With kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: VALUATION AREAS 01 AND 51 DO NOT ACCOUNT HOMOGENEOUSLY
entry.content:
When a new item is added to an already created asset account due to a purchase, the depreciation of the asset and the capital grant are consistent. In both cases, they depreciate the same amount, which is what the system should do. The problem identified arises when the addition is due to a fixed cash transfer using the ABUMN transaction. The depreciation amounts are not the same when they should be.

The conclusion is that both cases should be the same. However, they are not, and the correct calculation would be the one performed with the capital subsidy, not the accounting department.

The difference is that area 51 calculates the amortization based on the reference date and the 01 from the following month, but that happens because the asset is old (from previous years)

In this case the calculation is the same because it is based on the reference date, as it is within the fiscal year.

More information: As the system is configured, both depreciation areas must begin calculating depreciation the following month (as area 01 does). Indeed, it was determined that depreciation calculation begins the following month. However, in the case of a late asset acquisition, depreciation would also begin the following month, but taking into account the reference period indicated for the asset. Therefore, the calculation would include the months of delay from when the acquisition should have been made. For assets corresponding to unclosed fiscal years, areas 01 and 51 act uniformly, and in the opposite case, they do not, hence the discrepancy that should not occur.

entry.title: Joule Setup for SAP Integrated Product Development
entry.content:
SAP Integrated Product Development (SAP IPD) is a cloud solution that enables you to digitally orchestrate your product development from design-to-operate, resulting in reduced time to market, higher R&D return on investment, higher margins, and reliable product launches. 

In the current released version, SAP IPD business users have conversational access to the SAP Integrated Product Development documentation on the SAP Help Portal.  

Joule is supported in the following SaaS applications (capabilities) for SAP Integrated Product Development. 

 

Core capabilities Supported Scope 

Use Joule in the following capabilities 

  • Collaboration 
  • Common Functions 
  • Configuration Management 
  • Engineering. Note: Joule is currently only supported by the apps for Test Management. 
  • Enterprise Product Structure 
  • Insights 
  • Intelligent Handover 
  • Product Data Integration 
  • Product Innovation Management 
  • Specification Management 
  • Visualization 
  • Formulation 

Supported Languages: Joule currently supports the following languages (see image below) 

Please refer to the link for the most up to date information on languages supported by Joule.  

It is also important to note that SAP Integrated Product Development can be activated in the data centers that are listed here, and when integrating the product with Joule, please note to validate the data centers supported by Joule here.  

*************************************************************************************************************************************************

This blog post is a series for Joule Activation: 

  • SAP Business AI – Overview for all !!! (Includes information on Joule Supported Data Centers, Different scenarios during Joule setup, etc..) 
  • Joule - Unified Setup: Bridging Simplicity and Performance (Demo Video to understand the Unified Setup, Joule basics & architecture, how to set up Joule, etc...)  
  • Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - A Comprehensive Setup Guide 
  • Joule Setup – End-to-End Setup Guide (for all Line of Business) Unified Approach 
  • Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - Setup Guide 
  • SAP Digital Supply Chain 
  • Joule for SAP Integrated Business Planning (SAP IBP) – Setup Guide 
  • Joule – Getting Started with Document Grounding - setup guide 
  • Activate Joule with SAP Build Work Zone and SAP Mobile Start 
  • Analytics Insights in Joule – Setup Guide 
  • Joule in SAP Signavio Process Transformation Suite – Setup Guide 

Joule in SAP Integrated Product Development – Setup Guide - You are Here 

Blogs - Work in Progress (WIP): 

  • SAP SuccessFactors Joule Activation (Refer to SAP Discovery Center) 
  • SAP Digital Supply Chain (WIP) 
  • SAP Asset Performance Management 
  • SAP Product Lifecycle Management 
  • SAP Digital Manufacturing 
  • SAP Concur 
  • SAP Ariba 
  • and more to be added towards General Availability 

*************************************************************************************************************************************************

Joule for SAP Integrated Product Development (you here) 

This blog will uncover the steps to activate Joule in two different ways (1) create a Joule for SAP IPD as a standalone product (Fresh activation) (2) activate and add Joule for SAP IPD with an existing subaccount along with your other activated / current LoBs landscape. 

Let's explore how to activate Joule for SAP IPD.  

Scenario 

Activate SAP's generative AI assistant, Joule, for SAP Integrated Product Development (SAP IPD) and understand the key factors that business users should consider when integrating Joule with SAP IPD.  

Use Case:  

Conversational search of SAP Help Portal documentation for SAP IPD: Your business users can ask Joule questions about the SAP IPD capabilities and processes, and Joule gives them an answer based on the SAP IPD documentation from the SAP Help Portal. This feature will provide quick access to user enablement content, summarizing answers from product documentation. It will save customers time by eliminating the need to browse through long search results and multiple topics. 

Pre-requisites 

  • You have an entitlement for Joule in the same global account on SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry, as your entitlement for SAP Integrated Product Development. 
  • You have subscribed to the Common Functions SaaS application. 
  • Your identity provider uses the OpenID Connect protocol. If you currently use a SAML protocol, see this information about migrating: Migrating from SAML to OpenID Connect Protocol 
  • Ensure that the EPD Admin role template is assigned to the user/admin working on the setup. For more information, see Role Templates for Common Functions. 

Integration Process: 

If you are activating Joule for the first time in your SAP Integrated Product Development system, we refer to this as a standalone/single setup, as mentioned in “Section 1”.  

In the standalone setup process,  

  • You may decide to use the existing IPD-Subaccount to activate Joule (check the data centers support for Joule), or  
  • You may plan to use a new subaccount dedicated to Joule, or  
  • If you would like to “add IPD” to an existing subaccount where Joule is activated, which is a unified setup as described below in “Section 2”.  

What does Unified Setup mean? 

The unified setup of Joule with multiple SAP products allows business users to ask any question irrespective of the Joule client they are in. This approach helps avoid toggling between multiple applications, improves user efficiency, and delivers a more consistent, connected experience across all LoBs. 

Check your setup plan: 

We hope you've selected one of the setup plans by now. Before we begin activating Joule for SAP IPD, let’s understand how it works. 

The image below explains how Joule works with your landscape integration: 

Note: In the current release of Joule for IPD, we do not use the SAP Build Work Zone and Cloud Foundry services. This is planned for the next releases.   

Now, since you have a clear understanding of IPD and how Joule works now it is time for us to start the integration process:  

Let us have a look at the steps involved for the integration: 

*************************************************************************************************************************************************

Integrating Joule  

  • Step 1: Setup Plan:  
  • Section 1: Fresh Setup – no existing Joule setup exists 
  • Section 2: You have an existing Joule subaccount, and you want to enable Joule for IPD 
  • Step 2: Post-integration checks 
  • Step 3: Joule / AI scenarios for IPD 2025 Roadmap 
  • Step 4: Joule test scenarios 

“Note: Always refer to the SAP Help Page for SAP Integrated Product Development to refer to the most up to date information. 

 

Step 1: Setup Plan 

Section 1: Fresh Setup – no existing setup exists 

If you already have some experience with SAP BTP (Business Technology Platform), we request that you follow the steps in this blog - Joule Setup – End-to-End Setup Guide (for all Line of Business) Unified Approach in this blog, and please check Section 2 to check your SAP BTP Account for Joule Entitlements. 

If this is your first time using the SAP BTP platform and you're unsure how to proceed with the activation, don’t worry - we’ve got you covered. Just follow the steps below to activate Joule for SAP IPD easily. 

  • Create the Subaccount  
  • Set up Trust Configuration with SAP Cloud Identity Service 
  • Add Entitlements 
  • Run the Joule Booster and see Joule in Action 

Step (1): Create the Subaccount  

  • Check this blog and follow step No. – 5 (refer to image below) and ensure you validate the Joule supported data centers.  

If you are looking at multiple LoB integrations with Joule in the future 

  • Check this blog and follow step No. – 4 (refer to image below) 

Step (2): Set up Trust Configuration with SAP Cloud Identity Service 

The complete setup process is documented in this blog. Step No. 7 explains the process. For simplification, we have the reference images below. 

This setup is done at your new subaccount. Navigate to Security -> click on Trust Configuration -> click on Establish Trust and follow the images below.  

 

Step (3): Add Entitlements 

The complete setup process is documented in this blog. Step No. 8 explains the process. For simplification, we have the reference images below. 

This completes the prerequisite setup at the SAP BTP Subaccount.  

Step (4): Run the Joule Booster and see Joule in Action 

The complete setup process is documented in this blog. Step No. 10 explains the process. For simplification, we have the simplified steps and images below. 

 

  • Click on Global Account -> Select Booster -> Search for Joule -> Run the Booster 

The system will validate the entitlements required. Once it’s green, you can click on Next 

  • Select the SAP BTP Subaccount and click on Next 

  • Select Integrations, you will be able to see the options for SAP IPD, select it, and click on Next 

 

Once you select the IPD System, you can click on Next, review the selections, and click on Finish. The booster will take 5-9 minutes to activate Joule and set up the integrations with your IPD system. 

Joule will now return the success message after the integrations are completed. You can validate this setup by navigating to Systems and Formation, searching for the Joule formation that will be created for your setup, as shown below.  

Now, we have completed the activation process. You can go to your IPD system and check for the Joule icon as shown below.  

 

Congratulations on successfully setting up the SAP IPD Standalone system! 

Now, let’s walk through how to add SAP IPD to your existing subaccount alongside your other active Lines of Business (LoBs). 

Step 1: Setup Plan 

Section 2: You have an existing Joule subaccount, and you want to enable Joule for IPD. In this option, we will show you how to activate IPD in the context of an existing Joule formation. Within your SAP BTP Global Account, navigate to System Landscapes, click on Formations 

Search for the Joule formation you have already set up. In my case, “I am using one of the existing formations, which has multiple SAP products connected to one Joule instance, as shown below”. You can click on Include Systems.  

 

In the new window, filter for SAP IPD and click on Next step.  

 

 

Select the option “Enable Capability Deployment” and “Enable the Joule Icon in Integrated System” and click on Review.  

 

Validate the selections and click on Include.  

 

 

The process may take 5 to 8 minutes to complete, and you should be able to see the synchronizing option, as shown in the image below.  

Once the status(synchronizing) turns green and Ready, we are good to use Joule with IPD capabilities. 

Congratulations on successfully setting up SAP IPD with the Unified Approach! 

Appendix:  

What is Planned ahead  

Keep an eye on some of the exciting AI scenarios that we have planned for the rest of the year. 

2509 (Q3’25) 

AI-assisted 2D hot spot creation  

  • Feature would be released as GA (2509). Tagging master data to a visual model in IPD is a manual cumbersome and time-consuming process. AI assisted hotspot creation will automatically tag the relevant business data to the 2D visual models, thereby improving efficiency and helping realize a true visual digital thread. 

AI-assisted document summary  

  • Feature currently available as “Cloud Feature Beta”, will be released as Embedded AI (without Joule in Q3’25). Subsequently, we plan to embed Joule to this scenario.  
  • Scenario will allow users to summarize complex engineering documents and provide a concise summary by using the power of LLM. This will provide benefits by saving time in manual reviews and data interpretation. 

AI-powered text generation for Innovation Management:  

  • Feature will be released as Embedded AI (without Joule in Q3’25). Subsequently, we plan to embed Joule to this scenario. This scenario will allow campaign managers and idea submitters to leverage AI-based generation of new ideas based on campaign details, thereby helping to sharpen the campaigns and generate ideas using tailored prompts thereby helping in faster flow of ideas and faster time to market for launching new products. 

AI-powered text generation for Requirements Management: 

  • Feature will be released as Embedded AI (without Joule in Q3’25). Subsequently, we plan to embed Joule to this scenario. This scenario will allow requirements engineers to leverage power of LLM thereby helping to improve the clarity, structure, and consistency of requirement specifications. It provides real-time grammar and language suggestions tailored to engineering content. This ensures that requirements are easier to write, review, and understand across technical and non-technical teams 

Features planned for 2512 (Q4’25) 

AI-assisted Product Specification Search (GA 2512) 

  • LLM powered specification search will take inputs in the natural language form and provide contextual results using the customer database (that may contain 100000-500000 specifications on an average). The system recognizes the content and builds filters based on the query and is also useful for people who may not even be familiar with the property itself that they want to search for. The functionality will help save information retrieval time by finding specific details about a product by searching in natural language, rather than navigating through complex databases or documentation. Additionally, by enabling quick access to relevant information, developers can spend more time on actual product development and less time searching for data, leading to faster project timelines. 

AI-assisted Product Recipe Search (GA 2512) 

  • Similar to Specification search, this use case would leverage the power of LLM and Joule to search for IPD Formulation customers searching for recipes in their database based on natural language prompts.  

AI-based natural language processing (NLP) to search for documents 

  • Feature would be released as Cloud Feature Beta AI-driven NLP system for document searches, enabling users to quickly find information using natural language queries Interpretation and processing of user queries to provide relevant search results 

Now, let’s explore the Joule test scenarios outlined below. 

  1. 4. Joule test scenarios Link: https://help.sap.com/docs/JOULE/82a14f108cfa4d4788244d81371e072b/2ff600db0ce74e3e9dda6d8d0d81d761.html?locale=en-US 

In a similar approach, you may use Joule with a unified experience in other SAP products that you have integrated in the same subaccount. 

If you have any questions about the setup, you can reach us at SAP_AI_RIG@sap.com  

Thank you for staying connected with our blog and successfully activating Joule for SAP IPD! 

We hope you’ve had an exciting experience exploring the power of Joule, your intelligent business companion. From insightful decisions to streamlined processes, we trust you’re already seeing the incredible value Joule brings to your day-to-day work. 

Here are smarter, faster, and more impactful outcomes powered by Joule! 🚀

entry.title: Re: SAP CO: Cost Centers CDS Extractors?
entry.content:
Hi,

CO-OM datasources are still supported in the S/4HANA system. Currently, no official document tells they'll be replaced by CDS view. See Central Note 2500202 - S4TWL - BW Extractors in SAP S/4HANA , open its attachment, and remove the filter on the 1st column, search for 0CO_OM_CCA_ or others, you'll the information "Whitelisted" + "DS working - no restrictions".

If you still want to find which CDS may be related (remember there's no 1:1 mapping between classical datasource and CDS for ALL areas), please read this blog:

Finding the right CDS Extractor in SAP S/4HANA - SAP Community

Hope the information above is helpful.

Regards,
Maggie

entry.title: Re: Payment Medium Format - Data Source in Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello @Jan-Niklas_S 

This is a known challenge when working with the pain.001.001.03 XML structure in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, especially when banks request two distinct <Id> values under <InitgPty> and <Cdtr>/<CdtrAgt> or <Dbtr>/<DbtrAgt> blocks

Your payment medium XML shows the same ID in two places (<InitgPty> and <OrgId>), but your bank wants two different IDs.

Where Each ID Comes From

  1. <InitgPty><Id> (Initiating Party ID)
    → Controlled in “Define Payment Medium Format Parameters” (SSCUI)
    → Field: Initiating Party ID
    → Or defaults from the “Customer Number” in Manage Bank Accounts
  2. <OrgId><Id> (e.g. Debtor/Creditor ID)
    → Controlled by:
    • The House Bank (field: Customer Number or Bank ID), or
    • The Business Partner bank details (field: Identification Number)

What You Should Do

  • Set two different values:
  • One in the Initiating Party field (payment method config)
  • One in the House Bank or BP master data

This will produce two separate IDs in the XML output.

Check SAP KBA 2559572

https://me.sap.com/notes/2559572

entry.title: AEM integration with S/4HANA On-Premise 2022 Initial Stack
entry.content:

I'm currently working on a migration from traditional Event Mesh to ADVANCED Event Mesh for an S/4HANA On-Premise 2022 Initial Stack implementation. The goal is to enable Enterprise Event functionality in this environment.

However, I’ve encountered a challenge:
🔹 The official SAP Help Portal only provides instructions for S/4HANA 2022 LATEST, but I haven’t been able to find any documentation or support for the Initial Stack version.

🔹 It seems there is no direct guidance on whether Enterprise Event support exists for S/4HANA 2022 Initial Stack, or if there’s an alternative approach to achieve this migration.

Has anyone successfully enabled Enterprise Event on S/4HANA On-Premise 2022 Initial Stack using Advanced Event Mesh?

💡 Any recommendations, workarounds, or insights would be greatly appreciated!
Looking forward to your suggestions!

Thanks in advance.

 

References: 

2022 - Initial: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/810dfd34f2cc4f39aa8d946b5204fd9c/fbfdeeab605349b7b1f0330ee4af253b.html?version=2022.000&locale=en-US

entry.title: Re: Connection tab keeps loading and fails "logon.faces"

entry.title: Program for KSB1 to run multiple variants together
entry.content:
Hi All

I am trying to write a wrapper program for KSB1 however I don't see any data after I execute the program. If I run KSB1 directly with the same variant I see the data. I want to run multiple variants together & save all the files separately on the given path preferably in xlsx format or csv/xls format. If this works I will add option to save files in either xlsx or pdf & then later on I will add option to send files via email through background job. I m functional consultant & learning ABAP.

 

TABLES: varid.

TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_variant,
         name TYPE raldb_vari,
       END OF ty_variant.

TYPES: ty_line255 TYPE c LENGTH 255.

DATA: lt_variant  TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_variant,
      ls_variant  TYPE ty_variant,
      lt_list     TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF abaplist,
      lt_text     TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_line255 WITH DEFAULT KEY,
      lv_fullpath TYPE string,
      lv_count    TYPE i.

SELECTION-SCREEN BEGIN OF BLOCK b1 WITH FRAME TITLE text-001.
SELECT-OPTIONS: s_varnt FOR varid-variant.
PARAMETERS: p_path TYPE rlgrap-filename OBLIGATORY.
SELECTION-SCREEN END OF BLOCK b1.

* Text-001 = 'KSB1 Wrapper - Multiple Variants to Local Excel'

START-OF-SELECTION.

  " Ensure program runs only in foreground
  IF sy-batch = 'X'.
    MESSAGE 'Program cannot run in background. Please run in foreground.' TYPE 'E'.
  ENDIF.

  " Get list of variants for report RKAEP000 (not RKSB0100)
  SELECT variant AS name
    INTO TABLE lt_variant
    FROM varid
    WHERE report = 'RKAEP000'
      AND variant IN s_varnt.

  IF lt_variant IS INITIAL.
    MESSAGE 'No variants found for RKAEP000.' TYPE 'E'.
  ENDIF.

  " Process each variant
  LOOP AT lt_variant INTO ls_variant.
    CLEAR: lt_list, lt_text, lv_fullpath.
    lv_count += 1.

    " Build file path
    lv_fullpath = p_path && '\' && ls_variant-name && '.CSV'.

    " Submit RKAEP000 with the variant, export list to memory
    SUBMIT rkaep000
      USING SELECTION-SET ls_variant-name
      EXPORTING LIST TO MEMORY
      AND RETURN.

    " Get list from memory
    CALL FUNCTION 'LIST_FROM_MEMORY'
      TABLES
        listobject = lt_list
      EXCEPTIONS
        not_found = 1
        OTHERS     = 2.

    IF sy-subrc <> 0.
      WRITE: / 'Could not get list for variant:', ls_variant-name.
      CONTINUE.
    ENDIF.

    " Convert list to ASCII format
    CALL FUNCTION 'LIST_TO_ASCI'
      TABLES
        listobject = lt_list
        listasci   = lt_text
      EXCEPTIONS
        OTHERS     = 1.

    " Download file to local system
    CALL FUNCTION 'GUI_DOWNLOAD'
      EXPORTING
        filename     = lv_fullpath
        filetype     = 'ASC'
        codepage     = '4110'
      TABLES
        data_tab     = lt_text
      EXCEPTIONS
        OTHERS       = 1.

    WRITE: / 'Saved variant', ls_variant-name, 'to', lv_fullpath.

  ENDLOOP.

Regards

Pankaj

entry.title: Re: Request for setting default field value in purchase o...
entry.content:
Hi @Baktim

Company code, purchasing organization, and purchasing group can be maintained as default values in User Default Settings.

However, these values are not automatically populated in the Purchase Order—whether using the Manage Purchase Orders app or the Create Purchase Order – Advanced app. The default values only appear after changing certain fields (e.g., document type or currency), which triggers the system to update them.

In the case of the Create Purchase Order – Advanced app, if values are maintained under Personal Settings, they are automatically populated without requiring any manual change or trigger.

Regards,

Vinith K

entry.title: Unlocking Business Value with AI and ISLM: Insights from SAP Sapphire 2025
entry.content:
Fresh from our experience at SAP Sapphire & ASUG Annual Conference  2025 in Orlando, we’re excited to share key highlights from our session.

 Together with @markus_winter, I had the opportunity to present how Intelligent Scenario Lifecycle Management (ISLM)—is accelerating innovation and transforming the way businesses operate with SAP S/4HANA.

I’m Priti Dhingra, Director - Product Management at SAP. Since joining SAP in 2001, I’ve had the opportunity to contribute across various roles and initiatives, partnering closely with customers and driving innovation across the SAP ecosystem. In my current role, I drive customer-facing initiatives in Cloud & Lifecycle Management, with a strong focus on AI lifecycle management.

Business AI Vision and Overarching Goals

Before we dive into the technical aspects, let's establish the overarching vision of SAP when it comes to AI. Our goal is to empower organizations to leverage SAP’s portfolio of technology to become intelligent, efficient, and sustainable. AI is not just a buzzword; it is a critical enabler for digital transformation. By embedding AI into our ERP and other business applications, we aim to automate processes and enable data-driven decision-making.

In our session:  Empower your business: Revolutionize SAP S/4HANA with AI and ISLM, we highlighted the latest advancements in ISLM and how they simplify the integration of Generative AI into SAP environments.

What is ISLM?

ISLM stands for Intelligent Scenario Lifecycle Management. It is a framework designed to seamlessly integrate AI into business applications. ISLM offers a comprehensive platform for managing the entire lifecycle of AI use cases, whether you are a customer, partner, or internal SAP development team.

Key Capabilities of ISLM

  • Integrated into SAP S/4HANA: ISLM is natively integrated into SAP S/4HANA and supports both embedded and side-by-side scenarios.
  • Pre-built AI Use Cases: ISLM supports lifecycle management of pre-built AI use cases.
  • Custom Use Case Creation: Customers and partners can use ISLM to build their own tailored AI use cases.
  • Support for Generative AI:  ISLM now supports generative AI use cases, acting as an integration component between applications and various large language models through the Generative AI Hub. Check out the detailed Blog post to learn more.

Benefits of Using ISLM

  • Simplified Integration: ISLM simplifies the integration and management of AI within the SAP ecosystem.
  • User-Friendly Interface: Designed for non-ML experts, ISLM abstracts technical complexities, making it easier to innovate with AI.
  • End-to-End Management:  ISLM supports the entire lifecycle of AI scenarios, from exploration to productive use.
  • Extensive Capabilities: Offers features like TurnKey AI for auto-enablement, data push management for remote ML providers, and support for multi-model management.

Conclusion 

ISLM is a powerful tool that simplifies the integration and management of AI within the SAP ecosystem. With support for both pre-built and custom AI use cases, including generative AI, ISLM empowers organizations to leverage the full potential of AI.

Call to Action 

If you are interested in exploring ISLM further, check out our:

Help center documentation

ISLM – SAP Community – Topic page

ISLM- Setup & Configuration

Webinar Replay : Integrate & Operate GenAI into SAP S/4HANA with Intelligent Scenario Lifecycle Management (ISLM)

Webinar Replay: Integrating Generative AI in SAP S/4HANA with ISLM

SAP Sapphire for me:  

It was a fantastic experience connecting—and reconnecting—in person with customers and stakeholders I’ve previously worked with virtually, while also building new relationships across the ecosystem.

Some of the most engaging conversations took place at:

📍MaxAttention Lounge – Strategic conversations around shaping AI roadmaps
📍Expert Center Talks – Deep dives into real use cases and solution architectures
📍ASUG Community Zone – A true sense of community, collaboration, and shared purpose

One recurring theme stood out in many of our discussions:
👉How can we make AI simpler, more accessible, and truly valuable for our customers?

The feedback we received was grounded, honest, and rooted in real business needs—reinforcing the importance of value-driven, easy-to-adopt AI solutions.

Let’s Stay Connected

One of the most rewarding parts of the event was connecting with so many passionate partners, customers, and colleagues—and hearing your real-world stories around AI. Let’s keep the momentum going!

👉Feel free to drop a comment and introduce yourself—I’d love to hear what you’re working on and how’s your AI journey shaping up so far?

entry.title: Clarification on Automatic Update of Requirement Date in Work Order Component Reservation
entry.content:
Description:

We would like to seek clarification regarding the system behavior for the requirement date (RESB-BDATU) when adding components to a maintenance work order.

Scenario:

  1. A maintenance work order is created on 29.08.2014.

  2. One operation (0010) is added along with two stock components.

  3. The order is saved and released, and the system creates reservations with the requirement date defaulted to 29.08.2014 (i.e., the basic start or operation start date).

  4. On 02.09.2014, the user adds another stock component to the same operation (0010).

  5. Upon saving, the system still sets the requirement date as 29.08.2014, even though the component was added on 02.09.2014.

Business Requirement:

The user expects that when a new component is added after initial order creation, the requirement date should default to the date on which the component is added (e.g., 02.09.2014), rather than the order's original start date — and this should happen automatically, without manual input.

Query:

Is it possible in standard SAP to achieve this functionality — where the requirement date for newly added components automatically reflects the current system date (SY-DATUM) at the time of addition, instead of defaulting to the order's basic/operation start date?

If not, please advise whether a standard configuration option or enhancement (user exit/BAdI) is recommended to meet this requirement.

System Info:

  • SAP Module: PM / MM

  • T-Code: IW31 / IW32

  • Relevant Table: RESB (Field: BDATU)

Kindly provide clarification or guidance.

entry.title: Validation to Restrict MIGO Posting to Blocked Storage Locations for Movement Types 561/101
entry.content:
Description:

We require a system validation during MIGO transactions to prevent posting to specific restricted P&M storage locations (e.g., 5000) using movement types 561 (Initial Stock Upload) and 101 (Goods Receipt against PO).

Business Scenario:

Certain storage locations such as 5000 are designated solely for P&M internal use and must not be used for direct stock uploads or goods receipts. However, users are currently able to post stock into these locations via MIGO, which leads to inaccurate stock management.

Transaction Involved:

  • MIGO – Goods Movement

Relevant Movement Types:

  • 561 – Initial Stock Upload

  • 101 – Goods Receipt against Purchase Order

Required Functional Behavior:

During execution of MIGO with movement types 561 or 101:

  • The system should validate the “To Storage Location” (LGORT).

  • If the storage location is 5000, the system should block the posting.

  • A custom error message should be displayed.

Sample Error Message:

“Posting to this storage location is not allowed.”

entry.title: Re: New Process for Changing Display Names in SAP Community
entry.content:
Hi, @Marian_Zeis:

As you probably noticed, names didn't change. 

RE: Why this process changed:

1) People can now create a username when they create an SAP account, so there's no need for them to create a separate username just for SAP Community.

2) Since the username is in the SAP Universal ID account manager, it can be made available to any SAP platform (such as ours).

3) Previously, the account manager would display first and last name by default. People can now create a username that gives them more anonymity (should they wish).

Kind regards,

--Jerry

entry.title: Re: How to do create with Odata V4 with Guid on Item with...
entry.content:
I never tried to create both the header and an item at once, only the header first and then the item. You can try the following:

const binding = oExtensionApi
    .getModel()
    .bindList("/MissionNeeds/MissionNeedsItem");
const payLoad = {
    "MissionNeedsID" : selectedLine.MissionNeedsID
    };

binding.create(payLoad);

// instead of attachCreateCompleted i use created
binding.created().then(() => {
    const itemList = oExtensionApi.getModel()
        .bindList("/MissionNeeds/MissionNeedsItem('" + selectedLine.MissionNeedsID + "')/_Item");
    const itemBinding = itemList.create(
        { 
            "MissionNeedsID" : selectedLine.MissionNeedsID, 
            "Product": selectedLine.Product, 
            "Quantity": "2.000", 
            "Unit": "ST",
        });
});

 However with your current Behavior Definition, this won't work because fields of your child entity being readonly. Only MissionNeedsID will be saved, the other fields will have default value. You can fix this by using readonly : update

field ( readonly : update )
  MissionNeedsItemID,
  MissionNeedsID,
  Product,
  Quantity,
  Unit,
  CreatedAt,
  CreatedBy,
  LastChangedAt,
  LocalLastChangedAt,
  LocalLastChangedBy;

entry.title: Re: F110 REGUH-XAVIS Separate Payment Advice indicator is...
entry.content:
This solves your issue.

1224437 - PMW: When are separate payment advice notes created?

A separate payment advice note is created by print, e-mail, or fax if:
- 'Always pyt adv' is entered in the company code for the payment method (transaction FBZP), Since the system determines only during data medium creation whether a separate payment advice note is required, payment advice note creation must take place after data medium creation. In this case, the value XAVIS in the table REGUH is set to 'X'. The payment advice note report RFFOAVIS_FPAYM processes only records with REGUH-XAVIS = 'X'. The field XAVIS can be 'X' only if Payment advice check box is active at Payment method at company code level in FBZP.

entry.title: Re: Bank Statement MT940
entry.content:
Hello @Chanaporn 

Thanks for the clear example and screenshot. You're working with MT940 bank statement integration in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, and your goal is to handle clearing logic between bank balance, technical, and client-specific GL accounts, particularly distinguishing Bank Incoming (1000002) and Bank Balance (1000001).

Let’s go through it

In Make Global Settings for Electronic Bank Statement, the system assigns:

  • GL account for symbol ++++++++30 = “Other Bank Transfer”
  • GL account for symbol ++++++++90 = “Technical Account”

This means,

  • These accounts are part of SAP’s standard posting model, used to balance entries during intermediate clearing in the MT940 process.
  • However, your custom account 1000002 (Bank Incoming) is not being hit because the system doesn’t see it as part of the auto-posting logic via the account symbols defined.

Solution Approach in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Since you're in Public Cloud, your configuration is limited to:

  • Using Account Symbols & Posting Rules
  • Managed in: “Make Global Settings for Electronic Bank Statement” (SSCUI ID: 100297)
  • You cannot build custom Z-logic, but you can adjust mapping if permitted.

 

Suggestions

  1. Check Account Symbol Mapping
  • Go to: Define Account Symbols
  • Add a new symbol for “Bank Incoming” (e.g. BANKIN)
  • Assign it to GL account 1000002
  1. Adjust Posting Rule
    • Go to: Assign External Transaction Types to Posting Rules
    • For incoming transaction code (e.g. NTRF, NTRI, etc.), assign a new posting rule that references BANKIN instead of +30
  1. Check Posting Specification
    • Ensure the correct combination of:
      • Bank Subaccount: 1000002 (Bank Incoming)
      • Bank Main Account: 1000001 (Bank Balance)
    • Use Manage Incoming Payment Files to simulate behaviour

Please note!

  • ++++++++30 and ++++++++90 are SAP-controlled logic for interbank clearing and should not be modified directly in the posting model.
  • However, you can build alternate account symbols + posting rules and assign them per External Transaction Code in MT940.

Summary

Goal

Solution

Use 1000002 for Bank Incoming

Create a custom account symbol & assign it in the posting rule

Avoid the system defaulting to & 30

Map your MT940 transaction to a new posting rule

Public Cloud restriction

No custom BAdIs work via SSCUI and posting rule logic only

entry.title: Re: Error "Duplicate column names in the result" with col...
entry.content:
Hi,

Thank you very much for your reply.

"So I am guessing that you are coding this SQL in a universe derived table.  Please correct me if I am wrong."
--> I don't use derived tables. Anyway, the error occurs with all kinds of tables (origin tables, alias tables, derived tables) in IDT and WebI (there is no error in BigQuery with exact same queries).

"When coding a derived table, as you mentioned, you need to provide a unique alias, like TABLE_1.COL_NAME as COL_NAME1.  Help me to understand why this would not be sustainable. "
--> This solution is not sustainable because I cannot add an alias through a custom SQL in WebI, I have to add the alias in the SQL definition of the object in the business layer of IDT (we have hundreds of documents with tens of queries each that encounter this problem).
Yet, I cannot use such objects (with an alias in their SQL definition) in the filter section in WebI, which is a major problem for us.

It seems that the problem is related to the connection driver, and/or the way IDT is configured (.prm files for instance).
Indeed, the problem did not occur on our previous platform BO 4.2 SP9 Patch 7 using a generic ODBC driver (which is not supported by SAP). We currently use BO 4.3 SP4 Patch 6 with native ODBC BigQuery driver.
Can you confirm that you use a native BigQuery ODBC driver as well?

Thanks in advance,

entry.title: What’s new in Mobile development kit client 25.6
entry.content:
Hello Community,

I am happy to announce that a new release of the Mobile Development Kit(MDK) is available for all Mobile Services customers and can be downloaded on the SAP Software Center and will soon be available on community Download page as well.

The SAP Mobile Services Client has also been updated to 25.6.0 both in the Google Play Store and in Apple App Store.

SAP Mobile Development Kit enables developers and technical business users to build cross-platform mobile applications. It allows you to build your application once, in an integrated development environment (SAP Business Application Studio / VSCode extension) and run it natively on Mobile (Android & iOS).  

Below enhancements are available only on iOS and Android platforms:

  • New UI enhancements
    •  Support Grouping in Object Table Control
    • Support Body Elements in Card Collection Control
    • Donut Chart Support
    • Support Customizing Placeholder in Form Cell List Picker Control
    • Bottom Sheet Support for Popover Menu (Android Only)
    • Support Top Caption in Form Cell Segmented Control (iOS Only)
  • API and Syntax enhancements
    • Support Lock and Wipe Settings in the MDK Client
    • Support Device Compliance in MDK Client
    • Expose Generate Content API from Gemini LLM
    • Simplify Application Launcher and Splash Screen Generation in MDK Branded Client
    • Support for getCell in DataTableRowProxy

Support Grouping in Object Table Control 

MDK now supports grouping items in an Object Table section, making it easier to get an overview of data on large list screens.

//excerpt of a page 
"Grouping": {
    "GroupingProperties": [
        "CategoryName"
    ],
    "Header": {
        "Items": [{
            "Title": "Category: {CategoryName} ",
            "Styles": {
                "Title": "DataTableGroupHeaderItem",
                "BackgroundColor": "sectionBgColor"
            }
        }]
    }
},
"_Type": "Section.Type.ObjectTable",
….

Grouping in Object Table control

For more information, refer to the Grouping documentation and its API Reference.

Support Body Elements in Card Collection Control

The Card Collection control provides brief, related pieces of information and serves as an entry point, teaser, or preview for more detailed content. By tapping on a card, users can select it and navigate to a dedicated page with expanded information.

We have now introduced Body Elements in the Collection control. The card body is the central part of the card, used to display additional information alongside the content shown in the card header. It is a separate container that can hold various components, with a flexible height to accommodate a reasonable number of UI elements.

Following contents can currently displayed in Card Body Elements currently supports:

  • Text
  • Label Bar
  • Space
  • Separator
//excerpt of a page
   "Body": {
        "_Type": "Card.Type.Body",
        "OnPress": "/MDK2504/Rules/Card/ShowToastMessage.js",
        "Contents": [
            {
                "_Type": "CardBodyContent.Type.Text",
                "Text": "The card body is the central part of the card that is used to display additional information alongside the content shown in the card header.",
                "NumberOfLines": 5
            },
            {
                "_Type": "CardBodyContent.Type.LabelBar",
                "Layout": {
                    "LayoutType": "Vertical"
                },
                "ItemSeparator": true,
                "Items": "/MDK2504/Rules/Card/CardStaticContentLabelBarItems.js"
            },
            {
                "_Type": "CardBodyContent.Type.Space",
                "NumberOfSpacings": 3
            },
            {
                "_Type": "CardBodyContent.Type.Separator",
                "_Style": "card-label-2"
            }
        ]
    },

Body Elements in Card Collection Control

For more information, refer to th Card Collection documentation and its API Reference.

Donut Chart Support  

You can now use the Donut Chart type to visualize data in a new way that emphasizes various aspects of the dataset. Donut charts are ideal for displaying proportions of categorical data in a part-to-whole analysis. They take a single dataset and represent the ratios in a circular format.

Support for the Donut Chart type is available in the Analytic Card Collection, Chart Content Section, and Object Header Chart view UI controls.

Donut Chart in iOS

Donut Chart in Android

For more information, see these documentation on Donut chart support in Analytic Card Collection, Chart Content Section, and Object Header.

Support Customizing Placeholder in Form Cell List Picker Control

We have introduced a new property, Placeholder, for the List Picker Form Cell control to customize the placeholder text when no value is selected. This property provides contextual cues to guide business users in making their selections.

 
//excerpt of a page 
{
    "_Type": "Control.Type.FormCell.ListPicker",
    "_Name": "FormCellListPicker0",
    "PickerPrompt": "Please select one single item",
    "PlaceHolder": "Select Item",
    "IsEditable": true,
    "PickerItems": [
        "Alpha",
        "Bravo",
        --
    ]
}​
 
 

For more information, see this  documentation.

Bottom Sheet Support for Popover Menu (Android Only)

A new property, IsBottomSheet, has been introduced for the Popver Menu Action.

  • When set to true, the popover is displayed as a bottom sheet.
  • When set to false or not defined, the popover is displayed as a dialog.
{
    "PopoverItems": [{
            "Icon": "sap-icon://synchronize",
            "OnPress": "/MDK2504/Actions/Service/SyncStartedMessage.action",
            "Title": "Sync Changes"
        }
    ],
    "_Type": "Action.Type.PopoverMenu",
    "IsBottomSheet": true
}

 

For more information, see this  documentation.  

Support Top Caption in Form Cell Segmented Control (iOS Only)

Prior to this release, MDK displayed segmented Form Cell controls on iOS as single-line buttons, which  would dynamically switch to a stacked layout. With this release, we are introducing a new property for the Segmented Control: CaptionPosition. When this property is set to Top, the control will always use stacked layout.

{
    "CaptionPosition": "Top", //"Adaptive"
    "_Type": "Control.Type.FormCell.SegmentedControl",
    "_Name": "FormCellSegmentedControl2",
    "Caption": "Top Caption",
    "ApportionsSegmentWidthsByContent": false,
    "Segments": [
        "Low",
        "Medium",
        "High"
    ]
}

For more information, see this  documentation.  

Support Lock and Wipe Settings in the MDK Client

We are adding support for Locking and Wiping settings on MDK client. This new feature will significantly improve security and protect business users' sensitive information, helping safeguard them against unauthorized access or misuse of their devices.

  • Lock (users): Lock users on the specified device and force them to re-authenticate to continue using the app.
  • Wipe (device registration) : Lock the user and clear all app data (including any local changes) on the device. User has to re-onboard to continue using the app.

 Administrator can lock users and wipe device registrations through the following options:

  • Initiate Locking or Wiping actions directly from the mobile services Admin UI using toggle buttons.

 

  • Set up offline locking/wiping policies that automatically lock or wipe the device when the configured number of days expires. Number of days are based on how long the app is offline (without connecting to server).

For more information, see this  documentation.  

Support Device Compliance in MDK Client

MDK introduces Device Compliance Detection, a security feature that allows app developers to perform various security checks to determine whether a device has been compromised.

Administrators must enable the Device Compliance Detection policies in the mobile services Admin UI to ensure compliance reports are sent to Mobile Services.  

When the Compliance Policy is enabled in the Mobile Services Admin UI:

  • Detection is automatically performed during app login and each time the app is restarted.
  • The compliance status for each registration will be visible in the Mobile Services Admin UI.

For more information, see this  documentation.  

To check the status of your device, we have added a new Client API isDeviceCompliant(). 

  • If it returns true, the device is compliant. 
  • If it returns false, the device is non-compliant or has been compromised.
var isRootDevices = clientAPI.isDeviceCompliant();

Support Generate Content API from Gemini LLM

In MDK’s previous release 24.11, we introduced support for leveraging the Chat Completion LLM API.

In this release, we are now introducing a new MDK Action called Generate Content, which connects to SAP AI Core and explicitly supports integration with Gemini Generate Content API, enabling seamless content generation. This API allows developers to create dynamic, structured content for articles, reports, and multimedia outputs, leveraging advanced AI models like Gemini 1.0 Pro and Gemini 1.5 Pro.

Instead of calling an LLM API provided directly by an LLM service provider, SAP AI Core offers a unified way to access LLM APIs from the most popular LLM service platforms. SAP AI Core manages the AI model and ensures scalable deployment in enterprise applications, maintaining compliance and security.

 The Generate Content LLM API can address various scenarios, including:

  • Text Input: Submit text prompts to the API to receive AI-generated responses.
  • Input: Enhance the interaction by including images, allowing for a multimodal conversational experience.
  • Function Calling: Enable the AI model to invoke specific functions or execute commands based on the conversation’s context.
{
  "_Type": "Action.Type.AICore.Generate.Content",
  "Target": {
    "Service": "/MDKDemoApp/Services/OpenAI.service",
    "Path": "/models/gemini-1.5-pro:generateContent"
  },
  "Properties": {
    "Contents": []
  },
  "ShowActivityIndicator": true,
  "ActivityIndicatorText": "Processing ...",
  "ActionResult": {
    "_Name": "GenerateContent"
  },
  "OnFailure": "/MDKDemoApp/Actions/Failure.action"
}​

For more information, see this  documentation.  

Simplify Application Launcher and Splash Screen Generation in MDK Branded Client

Prior to this release, the effort required to customize Application Launcher icon and Splash screen is non-trivial. A developer has to generate these icons and screens using different ways and different sizes for different platforms as documented here.

We have simplified and unified generating these application launcher icon and Splash screen for developers to provide these images under a single location.

A new property, AppImages, has been introduced in MDKProject.json , where you can mention the file details in the given properties.

"AppImages": {
  "Launcher": {
    "Logo": "launcher",
    "Background": "android_launcher_background", // Android only
  },
  "Splash": {
    "Logo": "splash_logo",
    "Background": "background", // iOS only
    "LogoBackground": "android_splash_background", // Android only
  }
}

All those files should be placed under yourMDKApp.mdkproject>App_Resources>MDK>Images

Please note you can still continue previous way of customization as mentioned in our documentation. However, in order to use this new way, we highly suggest to remove any customization or override files added for the launcher and splash screens in the App_Resources and App_Resources_Merge folders for both iOS and Android.

For more information, see this  documentation.  

Support for getCell in DataTableRowProxy

With this release, we are introducing getCell feature in DataTableRowProxy, enabling you to retrieve a DataTableCellProxy object using the column index of the target cell. This new functionality will seamlessly let you access and interact with specific cells within a table row.

let cellParent = context.getParent();
let columnIndex = context.getColumnIndex();
let getCell = cellParent.getCell(columnIndex).getValue();

New to MDK development?
Follow  these tutorials  to get started and learn more about Mobile development kit!
I am looking forward to your feedback/comments.

entry.title: Criterio al articulo 29 de la Ley del INFONAVIT
entry.content:
Buen día,

Se implementaron las notas del articulo 29 de la Ley del INFONAVIT
1.-Se modifico la  tabla-V_T5F99K2

32 CKINF DESCUENTO DIAS PROPORCIONAL - INFONAVIT  0 No diminuir ausentismos \ incapacidades

2.-Se modifico la  tabla-V_T51P6
/381 Apor. crédito INFONAVIT 05/12/2025 12/31/9999 B 3 4

3.-Se copio la regla MXIN a ZXIN y se agregaron las reglas que indica la nota


P0370 Cálculo deducciones crédito INFO/FONA
PIT ZFM0 Grabar Z381 antes de PRDNT
PRDNT
PIT ZFM1 Cambiar Z381 despues de PRDNT
PIT ZFM2 Cambia /381 y /385

 

Se realizaron , las pruebas y funciona cuando el mes de la  fecha inicio y mes de la fecha din son iguales.

Si el mes de la fecha inicio es diferente al mes de la fecha fin marca el siguiente error.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

CC-nómina no se ha reducido correctamente
Una CC-nómina no ha sido reducida completamente por importe
en tabla DDNTK
CC-nómina /381
Verifique si CC-nómina en evento de función PRDNT
todavía está en tabla IT (Clase de tratamiento 20)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

¿A alguien mas le marco este error y como lo solucionaron?

 

Gracias.

Saludos.
